↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 BIG | According to Reuters, a rescue mission in Iran nearly failed after two special operations aircraft (MC-130 Commando II), used for infiltration, suffered mechanical failures (reportedly damaging their landing gear during landing on a makeshift field airstrip) and were unable to take off. 🔹 This put part of the rescue force at risk of being stranded inside Iranian territory, a moment one senior U.S. official described as truly “shocking.” Due to time constraints and the growing risk of exposure, U.S. command decided to deploy additional aircraft to carry out a phased evacuation of the unit. 🔹 Reports indicate the rescue team had to wait several tense hours before the backup plan was successfully executed. 🔹 After the evacuation was completed, U.S. forces destroyed the two damaged MC-130 Commando II aircraft on site, along with four MH-6 Little Bird helicopters aboard them, to prevent sensitive equipment from falling into enemy hands. ▪️ Under the cover of darkness, around 100 U.S. special operations troops penetrated deep into Iranian territory, moving through difficult mountainous terrain south of Isfahan. ▪️ They crossed a ridge approximately 7,000 feet (about 2,100 meters) high to reach the stranded airman and successfully establish contact. ▪️ The F-15E Strike Eagle crew member, a weapons systems officer, had a sprained ankle and was hiding in a crevice near a mountain peak, using survival and evasion techniques after ejecting. ▪️ Before the rescue forces initiated direct action, he confirmed his identity to ensure U.S. troops would not fall into an Iranian trap. ⛑️ On the third day, the Central Intelligence Agency located the pilot in Isfahan Province. U.S. strike aircraft conducted attacks on Iranian columns to prevent them from approaching the pilot’s location. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇷🇺 Iran’s ambassador to Russia, Kazem Jalali, proposed a new legal framework for the Strait of Hormuz, stressing that traffic control is exclusively within the competence of the littoral states. He said the strait, which he likened to “Moses’ staff,” has become a real capability for Iran following the US‑Israeli aggression. Jalali suggested that transit of ships, whether oil or non‑oil, should be subject to bilateral agreements between Iran and the vessel’s flag state. Fees, credit lines to guarantee imports and exports, and assurances for purchasing Iranian products outside the current sanctions regime could be part of the arrangement. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇦 🇷🇺 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 i24NEWS: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy has accused Russia of providing Iran with intelligence on Israeli energy infrastructure, including satellite imagery and coordinates of 55 targets. According to a Ukrainian intelligence report, the list includes critical power plants, urban and industrial facilities, and local energy sites. The Orot Rabin power plant in Hadera is named as a primary target. Russian Ambassador to Israel Anatoly Viktorov rejected the claims in the i24NEWS report, stating that Russia and Israel maintain established security dialogue and that the allegations are artificially inflated. He added that Zelenskyy is spreading false information to divert international attention from the situation around Iran. @wfwitness
Netanyahu urged Trump in a call yesterday not to pursue a ceasefire now. Source: Barak Ravid

⚡️ 🇵🇰 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 Kan: Pakistan is not giving up on the possibility of brokering a ceasefire, even a short one, between the U.S. and Iran. Talks are being held with White House envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, and Washington has not ruled out a direct meeting between the parties. However, significant gaps remain, with Trump insisting on zero uranium enrichment in Iran – a demand Tehran rejects. Israeli officials are awaiting Trump’s ultimatum and expect a green light to strike Iranian energy and national infrastructure targets, albeit with restrictions. A Israeli security cabinet discussion is scheduled for tonight. @wfwitness

Gulf states are pressing the Trump administration for security guarantees in any agreement. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 i24NEWS: An IDF engineering and paratrooper force attempted to cross the Litani River near Beaufort Castle but encountered heavy resistance, leaving the force trapped for hours. They later withdrew, abandoning their equipment. IDF Sergeant Moshe Yitzhak Hacohen Katz was killed and about 20 soldiers were wounded in a heavy mortar and rocket barrage during the clashes. The Yahalom unit retreated, leaving behind engineering equipment and rubber boats, while the 890th paratrooper battalion held the area under fire for over an hour before receiving orders to disengage. Hezbollah later released footage of the abandoned equipment. The IDF said most equipment was recovered. @wfwitness
